channel.title: AI Research Weekly
channel.description: Weekly roundup of AI and machine learning research

item.title: Scaling Laws Revisited: New Findings on LLM Efficiency
item.description: Researchers at a leading AI lab have published updated scaling law estimates suggesting that compute efficiency improvements are outpacing earlier predictions. The study analyzed 150 model training runs across three orders of magnitude.

item.title: Agentic Systems in Production: Lessons from Six Months of Deployment
item.description: A retrospective on deploying multi-agent pipelines in production environments. Key findings include failure mode taxonomy, cost control patterns, and strategies for maintaining determinism across agent handoffs.

item.title: Open-Source Alternatives to Proprietary Embedding Models
item.description: A benchmark comparison of open-source embedding models against proprietary alternatives. Results show competitive performance on retrieval tasks at a fraction of the inference cost for high-volume applications.

item.title: Retrieval-Augmented Generation: When RAG Hurts More Than It Helps
item.description: An analysis of RAG failure modes in production systems, including stale retrieval index issues, chunking strategy mismatches, and query-document semantic gaps that cause RAG pipelines to perform worse than vanilla LLM prompting.
